name: cloak
description: MCP-native secrets vault. The model never sees plaintext secrets.
long_description:
Cloak replaces the anti-pattern of pasting API keys into prompts with a hardened local daemon that never exposes raw secret material to the model. Agents call action-shaped MCP tools (sign_request, proxy_authenticated_http_request, mint_short_lived_token) and the daemon performs the privileged operation on the agent's behalf. Reveal is a deliberate biometric-gated CLI act, not a tool call.

This extension bundles the cloak-mcp shim. It requires the cloak CLI + cloakd daemon to be installed separately (see https://cloakward.dev/install). On first activation the extension runs `cloak setup` which walks you through vault init, daemon launch, and biometric/passphrase prompts via your OS-native dialog flow — no terminal commands required.

tools.name: vault.list
tools.description: List secret names. Never returns secret values.

tools.name: tool.sign_request
tools.description: HMAC-SHA256 / AWS SigV4 signing. Returns only the signature header.

tools.name: tool.proxy_http
tools.description: Authenticated HTTP request via the daemon, allowlisted hosts only.

tools.name: tool.mint_token
tools.description: Short-lived token mint (e.g. AWS STS GetSessionToken).

tools.name: tool.query_audit
tools.description: Filter the hash-chained audit log. Never returns secret values.

tools.name: tool.policy_check
tools.description: Dry-run a tool invocation against the policy engine.
